content.description: Development context and conventions for the TaskFlow Pro AI-powered task management platform. This document captures all project-specific conventions, AI coding guidelines, and technical decisions that guide day-to-day development.

namingConventions.types: PascalCase with descriptive suffixes (e.g., TaskCreateInput, PriorityScoreResult)
namingConventions.files: kebab-case for utilities, PascalCase for components (e.g., priority-utils.ts, TaskCard.tsx)

gitConventions.prProcess: All PRs require 1 approval, passing CI, and linked story reference

testingConventions.unitTests: Vitest with >80% coverage target per story

frontend.stateManagement: Zustand for client state, TanStack Query for server state
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom TFDS design tokens

backend.cache: Redis 7 for session management and priority score caching
backend.queue: BullMQ for async task processing and integration sync jobs

ai.framework: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for ML service
ai.model: Fine-tuned transformer model (DistilBERT base) for priority scoring
ai.serving: ONNX Runtime for inference, model served via gRPC
ai.training: PyTorch with Weights & Biases for experiment tracking

codeGeneration: Always use TypeScript strict mode - no 'any' types except in explicitly typed escape hatches
Prefer composition over inheritance for React components
Use Zod schemas for all API input/output validation - never trust client input
All async operations must have proper error handling with typed error responses
Database queries must use parameterized queries via Drizzle ORM - never raw SQL concatenation
AI priority scores must include an 'explanation' field for transparency
securityGuidelines: Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) - use structured logging with field redaction
All API endpoints require authentication via JWT middleware
Rate limiting on all public endpoints: 100 req/min for authenticated, 20 req/min for unauthenticated
Input sanitization for all user-provided text fields to prevent XSS
CORS restricted to known frontend origins only
performanceGuidelines: API responses must be < 200ms p95 for CRUD operations
AI priority scoring must complete within 500ms for up to 100 tasks
React components must not re-render unnecessarily - use React.memo for list items
Database queries must use appropriate indexes - no full table scans in production

keyDecisions.decision: ADR-001: Use Zustand over Redux for client state management
keyDecisions.rationale: Simpler API, smaller bundle size, better TypeScript inference. Redux's middleware ecosystem not needed for our use case.

keyDecisions.decision: ADR-002: Separate ML service from main API via gRPC
keyDecisions.rationale: Allows independent scaling, different deployment cadence, and language flexibility (Python for ML, TypeScript for API). gRPC chosen over REST for lower latency and streaming support.

keyDecisions.decision: ADR-003: Use PostgreSQL over MongoDB for primary database
keyDecisions.rationale: Strong relational model fits task-project-user relationships. JSONB columns available for flexible schema needs. Better tooling and team expertise.

environmentSetup.quickStart: docker compose up -d && npm install && npm run dev
envVariables: ML_SERVICE_URL - gRPC endpoint for ML service
OPENAI_API_KEY - Optional, for AI explanation generation

additionalNotes: BMAD Interaction Rule: All BMAD workflows marked interactive MUST be run as interactive sessions — halt for user input at every step boundary.
BMAD Interaction Rule: Do NOT produce a complete artifact or implementation plan in a single response. Follow BMAD skill instructions for sequential step-by-step execution.
BMAD Interaction Rule: When a workflow step says STOP and WAIT or uses <ask> tags, halt and wait for the user's response before continuing.
BMAD Interaction Rule: Present checkpoint menus ([a] Advanced Elicitation [c] Continue [p] Party-Mode [y] YOLO) exactly as defined and halt for user selection.
BMAD Interaction Rule: Only skip interactive checkpoints when the user explicitly chooses YOLO mode.
